channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Sinner and Sabalenka answer doubters on Wimbledon Day one
item.description: Naomi Osaka impresses with her outfit and performance, while British hopes are dashed on first day of championship.

item.title: LIVE: Netherlands vs Morocco – FIFA World Cup 2026
item.description: Follow the updates, with live build-up, team news coverage, and our text commentary stream of the last-32 match.

item.title: Marco Rubio meets Libyan commander as US pushes to resolve Libya crisis
item.description: The US top diplomat commends Libyan leaders' efforts to 'overcome divisions' after talks with LNA's Saddam Haftar.

item.title: Venezuela earthquake victims shelter at golf course
item.description: Hundreds of Venezuelans are sheltering at a golf course in La Guaira after last week's deadly earthquakes.

item.title: Israel’s Smotrich calls for settlements, ‘conquest’ of Gaza Strip
item.description: Israeli Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich calls for the immediate establishment of Jewish settlements in the Gaza Strip.

item.title: What’s behind the anti-corruption crackdown in Iraq?
item.description: A number of senior politicians have been detained in a wave of arrests.

item.title: Martinelli scores late as Brazil beat Japan 2-1, into World Cup last 16
item.description: Martinelli's stoppage-time goal in Houston takes the five-time champions to the Round of 16, breaking Japanese hearts.

item.title: Deadly heatwave grips Italy and Balkans as wildfire fears grow
item.description: Older people remain most at risk as the heatwave drives up excess deaths across Europe.

item.title: Israeli attacks kill eight people in Gaza and a teenager in the West Bank
item.description: Israeli rights group B’Tselem has condemned the 'unprecedented killing of Palestinian children and teenagers"

item.title: Pakistani strikes kill dozens of Afghan villagers
item.description: Afghan villagers inspected destroyed homes after overnight Pakistani strikes targeting Taliban-allied fighters.

item.title: Israel launches attacks and incursions inside southern Syria
item.description: Israeli forces have shelled a village in southern Syria, firing artillery and illumination flares overnight.

item.title: ‘Miracle’: Man pulled from rubble four days after Venezuelan quakes
item.description: A 21-year-old man was pulled alive from the rubble of an apartment building in La Guaira, Venezuela.

item.title: Undocumented migrants flee South Africa amid rising anti-immigrant protests
item.description: Thousands of undocumented migrants in South Africa are rushing to leave after anti-immigrant protests.

item.title: Burnham pledges devolution and discipline if he becomes UK prime minister
item.description: The frontrunner to succeed Keir Starmer was criticised for not taking questions after setting out his policy vision

item.title: Can governments fix the global housing crisis?
item.description: From city skyscrapers to sprawling slums, a roof over your head has never been harder to afford.

item.title: UK’s prime minister-in-waiting vows to shake up politics
item.description: Andy Burnham, Britain’s likely next leader, vowed to enact radical change to the nation’s politics during a speech.

item.title: New Israeli assault in southern Syria forces families to flee their homes
item.description: Turkiye has condemned Israel’s attacks in Syria, calling them a violation of international law.

item.title: Mehdi Hasan: Disrupting democracy’s decline
item.description: With democracy on the decline in both the UK and US, Mehdi Hasan makes the case for independent journalism.

item.title: US Supreme Court backs Trump’s firings; Fed’s Cook reserved as exception
item.description: Justices rule in favour of expanded executive power, preserving Lisa Cook’s Fed position amid Trump’s dismissal attempt.

item.title: Venezuela quakes death toll rises to 1,719, thousands still missing
item.description: Tens of thousands remain missing after the devastating twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela last week

item.title: Ex-NBA players Malik Beasley, Ed Davis indicted in gambling case
item.description: Beasley, Davis and an NBA agent are among six defendants indicted on charges related to alleged sports betting plot.

item.title: Afghan families mourn loved ones as border tensions with Pakistan rise
item.description: Afghan officials say Pakistani attacks killed at least 36 civilians, while Pakistan says its forces killed 29 fighters.

item.title: US petrol prices ease despite ongoing uncertainties with Iran
item.description: US–Iran peace talks ease market volatility, but uncertainty keeps oil prices hovering, complicating recovery efforts.

item.title: US Supreme Court rebuffs Trump’s appeal in E Jean Carroll case
item.description: The US Supreme Court has rejected a push by President Trump to throw out a jury's findings of sexual assault.

item.title: Iraq’s corruption crackdown is a start, not a solution
item.description: New PM Ali al-Zaidi's arrest of 47 officials is welcome, but the rot runs through the system that put him in power.
